content.text:
*long pause*

*voice changes texture, quieter, slightly different quality*

There was this... my dad had this thing where he'd say — not meanly, he wasn't mean about it — he'd say "finish your work first." Like that was just the rule. Homework before TV, chores before anything fun. Which is fine, that's normal parenting.

But I remember one summer I wanted to take this drawing class. I was maybe eleven. And I asked him and he just got this look like... like it was almost painful for him. And he said we'd see. And we never saw. And I don't think it was even that expensive.

*pause*

And I don't think he said no because he didn't care. I think he said no because he felt like he couldn't. And that felt like — 

*stops*

I haven't thought about that drawing class in probably thirty years.

*quiet*

He wasn't withholding. He just... he couldn't give me that. And somehow I think I learned that you have to get to a certain place before you're allowed to want things. Before it's okay to do the thing that's just for you.

*very quiet*

That's not about 1.2 million dollars, is it.
